page: Amazon Web Services x2iedn.8xlarge is a memory-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8375C processor with an x86_64 architecture, providing 32 vCPUs across 16 dedicated physical cores with 2 threads per core. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz and is supported by a 54 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 1024.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 32 GB per core. It also includes a bundled 950 GB NVMe SSD local instance store and a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates average performance across most compute and memory metrics. The instance achieved a Geekbench single-core score of 1608.0 and a multi-core score of 13819.0, alongside a Passmark CPU score of 36913.69. Memory bandwidth tests show 1664.99 GB/sec for cached small-block reads, dropping to 138.74 GB/sec for large-block uncached reads and 62.28 GB/sec for large-block writes. In database-specific workloads, the server processed 31.04 million Redis SET operations per second and scored 14866.21 on Passmark database operations.

While the instance demonstrates strong text generation performance for small LLM models (135M) at 298.2 tokens/sec, it exhibits significant performance degradation on larger models. Large (70B) model inference drops to a weak 6.27 tokens/sec for prompt processing and a poor 2.49 tokens/sec for text generation, reflecting the lack of dedicated GPU hardware. Given the high memory density and local NVMe storage, this instance is designed for memory-intensive workloads, large-scale databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications requiring substantial RAM capacity without requiring GPU acceleration.
description: The Amazon Web Services x2iedn.8xlarge is a memory-optimized server powered by the Nitro hypervisor and an Intel Xeon 8375C processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, it delivers 32 vCPUs across 16 physical cores at 3.5 GHz. The hardware profile is defined by 1024.0 GB of DDR4 memory, providing a high density of 32.0 GB per core, alongside a bundled 950 GB NVMe SSD local instance store and 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks show average CPU and memory performance, with a Passmark CPU score of 36913.69 and 31.04 million Redis SET operations per second. However, without dedicated GPUs, LLM inference on large 70B models is poor, yielding only 2.49 tokens/sec for text generation. This instance is suited for memory-intensive databases, caching layers, and enterprise workloads requiring massive memory capacity.
og_description: Amazon Web Services x2iedn.8xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 32 vCPUs, 1024 GB DDR4 RAM, a 950 GB NVMe SSD, and 25 Gbps network bandwidth for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services x2iedn.8xlarge offers 32 vCPUs, 1024 GB DDR4 RAM, and a 950 GB NVMe SSD for memory-intensive database workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized server featuring one terabyte of system memory, dedicated Intel Xeon processing, and local NVMe SSD storage.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8375C processor with 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores
1024.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing 32.0 GB per core
Bundled 950 GB NVMe SSD local instance store
Suited for large-scale databases and memory-intensive enterprise applications
Poor performance for large-scale LLM inference due to lack of GPU acceleration
